label: Si: one does, one sells
description: The impersonal si (si mangia bene = one eats well) and the si passivante (si vendono libri = books are sold), plus the sign forms (Affittasi) and telling this si apart from its reflexive twin.

label: Impersonal si
description: General statements with no stated subject: si + third person singular.

label: Si mangia bene: the basic impersonal
description: Si + third person singular for what people in general do: In Italia si cena tardi. Si può fumare qui? Covers the very frequent si può / si deve / non si sa patterns.

attributes.common_miss: Plural verb with no plural noun ('si cenano tardi'), and English-calqued 'uno mangia' or 'tu mangi' where si is idiomatic.

label: Si è stanchi: the strange agreements
description: Two quirks at once: predicate adjectives after impersonal si go plural (quando si è stanchi, si dorme male), and compound tenses always take essere (si è parlato di politica), with the participle going plural for verbs that themselves take essere (si è arrivati tardi).

attributes.common_miss: Singular adjective ('quando si è stanco'), avere in the compound ('si ha parlato'), singular participle with essere verbs ('si è arrivato tardi').
canonical_examples: Quando si è stanchi, si dorme male.
Ieri si è parlato di politica.

label: Si passivante
description: Si with a transitive verb and a noun: the noun is the grammatical subject and the verb agrees with it.

label: Si vendono libri: agreeing with the noun
description: The verb agrees with the thing: si affitta una camera but si affittano camere. The same number-agreement instinct as c'è / ci sono, and missed the same way.

attributes.common_miss: The singular with a plural noun ('si vende libri'), extremely common even among natives in speech.
canonical_examples: In quel negozio si vendono libri usati.

label: Passivante or impersonal? When si agrees
description: With a transitive verb and its noun, si is passivante and agrees (si mangiano gli spaghetti); with an intransitive verb, an adverb, or no object, it is impersonal and stays third singular (si mangia bene). Deciding which frame you are in is the skill.

attributes.common_miss: Agreement forced where there is nothing to agree with ('si mangiano bene qui').
canonical_examples: Si mangiano gli spaghetti al dente.

label: Si in the wild
description: Where these constructions actually live: signs and small ads, and next to the reflexive si they must be told apart from.

label: Affittasi, cercasi, vendesi
description: The sign register glues si to the verb: Affittasi appartamento, Cercasi cameriere, Vendesi. Recognition matters more than production; the forms are frozen and usually stay singular whatever follows.

attributes.common_miss: Not recognising the attached si at all, and reading cercasi as a verb form of some other verb.

label: Which si is this?
description: Reading si correctly: reflexive with a subject (Marco si lava), impersonal or passivante without one (si lava tutto a mano), and the accented sì (yes) as a spelling matter. A recognition skill that keeps sentences from collapsing.

attributes.common_miss: Reading impersonal si as reflexive ('si mangia bene' as 'he eats himself well'), and si/sì accent slips in writing.
canonical_examples: Si lavano a mano. (either, context decides)
